content:
# Jafurah Sub-basin

**Summary**: A sedimentary basin located east of the Ghawar field.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] focused on the Jafurah Sub-basin for unconventional exploration.

- [[2018-aleid|Aleid, 2018]] located the study within the Jafurah Basin to evaluate local shale plays.

- [[2022-al-alawi|Al-Alawi, 2022]] studied the Jafurah Sand Sea to understand its geomorphology and sedimentology.

- [[2020-usman|Usman, 2020]] studied the carbonate mudrocks within the Jafurah sub-basin.

- [[2021-lawal|LAWAL, 2021]] studied the Jafurah sub-basin as a primary target for shale gas and oil development.

- [[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022]] Referenced in this thesis.

## Geological and Structural Setting

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] intra-shelf sub-basin

- [[2018-aleid|Aleid, 2018]] located the study within the Jafurah Basin to evaluate local shale plays.

- [[2022-al-alawi|Al-Alawi, 2022]] studied the Jafurah Sand Sea to understand its geomorphology and sedimentology.

- [[2020-usman|Usman, 2020]] studied the carbonate mudrocks within the Jafurah sub-basin.

- [[2021-lawal|LAWAL, 2021]] studied the Jafurah sub-basin as a primary target for shale gas and oil development.

- [[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022]] Referenced in this thesis.

## Formations Present

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] Tuwaiq Mountain, Hanifa, Jubaila

## Key Findings From This Location

- The basin is considered a new frontier unconventional play equivalent to the Eagle Ford in Texas. ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 16]])

- The Tuwaiq Mountain formation within this basin is a key target for gas production. ([[2018-aleid|Aleid, 2018, p. 23]])

- The Jafurah Sand Sea covers approximately 40,000 km2 and is characterized by Barchanoid dunes and a high humidity belt. ([[2022-al-alawi|Al-Alawi, 2022, p. 37]])

- The basin contains multiple organic-rich source-rock formations, including those of Kimmeridgian age. ([[2020-usman|Usman, 2020, p. 21]])

- The basin contains three organic-rich source rocks: Tuwaiq Mountain, Hanifa, and Jubaila Formations. ([[2021-lawal|LAWAL, 2021, p. 19]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022]])
